channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: After LPG shortage, will piped gas supply be next in India?
How 'dark mode' helped oil tanker reach Indian shores from volatile Strait of Hormuz
Case against Hardik Pandya for insulting Tricolour. What the law says about handling the flag
Why oil prices touched $100 a barrel despite announcement of historic reserves release
24,000 posts and counting… How anti-India hate is taking over US
Iran war: How much has fuel cost risen in Pakistan? How does it compare with India?
Iran’s brinkmanship and Trump’s redline: How the crisis is reshaping India’s West Asia strategy
item.description: If escalation hardens into a binary alignment, India’s pivot will be quiet but decisive. Strategic autonomy remains New Delhi’s doctrine, yet autonomy operates within the boundaries of national interest

item.title: Nehru’s frontier folly: How Bharat lost NWFP and gave Pakistan ‘strategic depth’
item.description: Bharat’s strategic marginalisation in Afghanistan did not begin with the rise of the Taliban in the 1990s; it actually started with a fateful decision during Partition

item.title: Secure yet exposed: India’s energy dilemma amid the West Asia crisis
item.description: India’s energy supplies remain secure in the short term due to reserves and diversified imports, but its heavy dependence on West Asian routes—especially the Strait of Hormuz—leaves it structurally vulnerable to prolonged geopolitical disruptions

item.title: Beyond Trudeau’s shadow: How Mark Carney charts an India-Canada reset
item.description: Moving past the tensions of the Justin Trudeau era, Carney’s India visit marks the end of a challenging period and the beginning of a more ambitious partnership

item.title: How the Iran war could radical reset the global order
item.description: The escalating confrontation between Israel, the US and Iran risks transforming a regional conflict into a crisis with profound global strategic and economic consequences

item.title: Trump’s Iran war could accelerate the global push against dollar hegemony
item.description: America has benefited immensely from the dollar’s hegemony, but first the Russia-Ukraine war, then the Venezuelan regime change, followed by claims over Greenland, and now the Iran war are all testing its dominance
